---
name: install-followup-statusline
description: Register (or remove) the /follow-up status bar — a live, agent-free 🔄 bar in Claude Code showing PR follow-up CI-wait progress (step + ✅🔴💬 counts) from monitor.js. Use when the user says "install the follow-up statusline", "show follow-up progress in the status bar", "register the follow-up status bar", or "remove the follow-up statusline".
argument-hint: "[--print | --remove]"
user_invocable: true
---

<!-- codex:plugin-root-preamble v1 — generated by scripts/codemod-plugin-root-preamble.js; edit the codemod, then re-run -->

> **Resolve the plugin root before running any script below.** A codex shell
> can inherit a stale, set-but-WRONG `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` from its launcher,
> so a bare `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-…}` fallback is NOT safe — validate the
> value against this skill's own SKILL.md first. Set `SKILL_MD` to this
> file's absolute path (codex lists it next to this skill in your skills
> list; under Claude Code the env var is always correct and the fallback
> branch never runs):
>
> ```bash
> SKILL_MD="/absolute/path/to/this/SKILL.md"   # from your skills list
> if [ -f "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-/nonexistent}/skills/install-followup-statusline/SKILL.md" ] &&
>    [ "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/install-followup-statusline/SKILL.md" -ef "$SKILL_MD" ]; then
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT"                         # validated — use as-is
> else
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$SKILL_MD")/../.." && pwd)" # stale/unset — self-locate
> fi
> ```
>
> Wherever this skill writes `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`, substitute the validated
> `$PLUGIN_ROOT` value (shells do NOT share variables between tool calls —
> re-resolve or inline the resolved path in each command).

# /follow-up status bar

Registers a Claude Code `statusLine` that renders live `/follow-up` progress —
agent-free. It reads the artifacts the plugin **already** writes — no new files:
the `.follow-up-orchestrator.pid` marker (located via the plugin's own
`findActiveMarker`, scoped to this Claude session) and `.follow-up-state.json`
(`currentStep`, `prNumber`, `_ciStatusParts` + `_monitorStartTime` — the
elapsed timer is recomputed live on every refresh, with `_ciStatusLine` as a
fallback for older state files) under `<TASKS_BASE>/<ticket>/`. When the last
persisted instruction (`.follow-up-next.json`) is `blocked`/`surface`, the bar
appends a `⚠ blocked` / `⚠ surface` marker so a workflow waiting on the
operator is visible at a glance.

This replaces the old per-poll stderr spam: the console is now near-silent
(only the final JSON instruction the agent acts on), and progress lives here.

Line format (one segment per actively-running follow-up in this worktree):

```
🔄 follow-up #<pr> · <step> · <status · N/40 · ✅ ╎ 🔴 ╎ 💬>
```

It is scoped to the session sitting in the PR's worktree, and registers through the
**shared statusline host** (`~/.claude/statusline-host.sh`) that owns Claude Code's
single statusLine slot and renders every bar (maestro 🎼, work ⚙, follow-up 🔄, qc 🔬).
Installing only drops the follow-up **fragment** — it never clobbers the other bars,
and the fixed host path means new tabs/sessions pick it up with no reinstall.

## Commands

- **Install:** `node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/workflows/follow-up/statusline/install-followup-statusline.js"`
- **Show resolved paths + current config:** `... install-followup-statusline.js --print`
- **Remove (drop just the follow-up bar):** `... install-followup-statusline.js --remove`

After installing, run `/reload-plugins` (or wait for the next refresh tick).

## How it fits together

- **Host:** `~/.claude/statusline-host.sh` — the single registered statusLine; renders
  every fragment under `~/.claude/statuslines/*.cmd` in filename order.
- **Fragment:** `~/.claude/statuslines/30-followup.cmd` — one line, the path to this
  plugin's renderer. Owned solely by the follow-up installer.
- **Renderer:** `scripts/workflows/follow-up/statusline/followup-statusline.sh` → `followup-statusline.js`.
- **Data source:** the plugin's existing `<TASKS_BASE>/<ticket>/.follow-up-state.json` + `.follow-up-orchestrator.pid` (no files created by the bar).
